highlights.text: I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My general lean is always to defer to the trend (because 90-95% of the time, price resolves with the trend, and we can’t predict the 5-10%).
Two of those core building blocks are 1) My core entry setup: The Failed Breakdown.
A core feature of Mode 2 is that is that price puts in extensive Failed Breakdowns.
As long as the triangle support holds (or failed breakdown and quick recover), ES can work back up the structure with 6301 being 1st target.

We put in a massive Failed Breakdown of the triangle around 11am, sold to 6241, trapped bears, we got long, and we got to triangle resistance at 6333 by Thursday, and broke it out, rallying to 6357 high of week last week.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a powerful variant of my core setup which I call the “double dip Failed Breakdown”.

post_title: Liftoff Day For SPX, But Can Bulls Keep The Momentum Up? July 22 Plan
